findings.criterion: Step implementations provide concrete setup, action, and DOM-based verification matching the Gherkin scenario (drag simulation via constructed DataTransfer + DragEvent; DOM assertions target #div1 .box2/.box3/.box1).

evidence.quote: When the user drags the product with data-testid "product-item-1" and drops it into the cart container with data-testid "drop-area"

findings.reasoning: All Gherkin steps have matching step definitions: the Given steps create a WebDriver and assert visibility via a parameterized data-testid; the When step calls drag_and_drop_html5 which constructs a DataTransfer and dispatches DragEvent events (dragstart/dragenter/dragover/drop/dragend); the Then steps perform DOM text assertions against #div1 .box2 (title), #div1 .box3 (price), and #div1 .box1 (quantity). The drop-target selector .box3 used for price matches the requirement that the unit price be presented in .box3. The test uses DOM observations (three DOM assertions) to confirm visible title/price/quantity, and the synthetic drag sequence is a plausible mechanism for triggering the application behavior.
